opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I swallowed some chicken and it feels stuck right behind my breastbone. I can't even swallow my own spit without it hurting, and I keep having to spit it into a bowl. This is really freaking me out.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It happened about two hours ago in the middle of dinner and it hasn't cleared — I can't get anything down, not even water or my own saliva.

symptom_narrative.onset: about two hours ago, halfway through eating dinner
symptom_narrative.duration: two hours and it hasn't budged
symptom_narrative.character: a stuck, blocked, pressure feeling behind the breastbone
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: really distressing — I can't swallow anything and I keep spitting
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: right in the middle of my chest, behind the breastbone
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since the food got stuck
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — even a sip of water just comes back up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to swallow makes the pressure worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I keep bringing up thick frothy saliva and I feel really anxious and worked up
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: over the last six months food has sometimes gone down slowly or felt like it caught, especially dry things like bread and steak, but it's never actually stuck like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't eat or drink, I'm pacing around and spitting into a container

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: an over-the-counter antacid now and then

objective_data_offered.value: on video, spitting thick frothy saliva into a bowl and unable to swallow it, able to talk in full sentences, keeps pointing to the middle of the chest
says he can breathe fine and is not short of breath
says his heart feels like it is racing
